_comment: Datapoint Naming Convention — canonical naming schema shared by all pipeline services. Every entity type has one canonical form; all source-specific aliases map to it. This file is the authoritative translation key used by CanonicalIDResolver and FieldTranslator.

naming_rules._comment: Rules every field name and entity identifier MUST satisfy before entering the KCRITR schema.
naming_rules.case: snake_case for all field names (e.g. kast_pct, NOT KASTpct, NOT KAST%)
naming_rules.separators: underscore only — no hyphens, dots, or spaces in field names
naming_rules.percent_suffix: Fields representing percentages end in _pct (e.g. kast_pct, headshot_pct)
naming_rules.count_suffix: Integer count fields use the bare noun or _count (e.g. kills, first_blood, clutch_wins)
naming_rules.id_suffix: UUID identity fields end in _id (e.g. player_id)
naming_rules.source_prefix: Source-specific raw fields MUST be prefixed before translation (e.g. vlr_gg.acs, liquipedia.kill_count)
naming_rules.canonical_prefix: Resolved canonical URIs use the cid: scheme (e.g. cid:player:tenz, cid:match:vlr/123456)
naming_rules.no_special_chars: Canonical entity names must be ASCII alphanumeric + underscore + hyphen only
naming_rules._max_alias_depth_note: An alias points to a canonical form — aliases cannot chain to other aliases

player._comment: A human player. Canonical form is the most widely-used competitive handle in lowercase.

match._comment: A single competitive match. Canonical ID uses the primary source URL slug.

map._comment: A playable map. Canonical names are Riot's official lowercase map names.

team._comment: A competitive team. Canonical form is the team's official tag in uppercase.

tournament._comment: A competition event. Canonical form is the official Riot designation.

agent._comment: A Valorant playable character. Canonical form is Riot's official name (Title Case).

field_translation_table._comment: Maps every source-specific field name to the KCRITR canonical field name. Used by FieldTranslator. Add new source columns here — never edit the KCRITR canonical names.

region.description: Geographic region code (NA, EMEA, APAC, BR, LATAM)

role.description: Combat role (Entry, IGL, Controller, Initiator, Sentinel)

kast_pct.description: % rounds with Kill, Assist, Survive, or Trade

patch_version.description: Game patch version at time of match

data_source.description: One of: vlr_gg, liquipedia, hltv, grid

checksum_sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of source HTML

vlr_gg._comment: VLR.gg HTML scrape field names → canonical KCRITR names

liquipedia._comment: Liquipedia API response field names → canonical KCRITR names

hltv._comment: HLTV API/scrape field names → canonical KCRITR names (CS2 pipeline)

grid._comment: GRID partnership API field names → canonical KCRITR names

conflict_resolution._comment: When two sources provide different values for the same canonical field on the same entity, apply these rules.
conflict_resolution._priority_note: vlr_gg is primary for Valorant; hltv is primary for CS2
conflict_resolution._store_both_note: On conflict, store the higher-priority value and log the discrepancy

deduplication_key._comment: The canonical dedup key is the stable UUID derived from the canonical IDs of player + match + map. This key is SOURCE-AGNOSTIC — the same player record from VLR and Liquipedia resolves to the same key.
deduplication_key._collision_policy_note: Two records with the same dedup key from different sources are treated as duplicates; the higher-priority source wins
